---
title: <small>Empirische Untersuchung eines Vier-Phasen-Unterrichtsmodells in Schweizer Sekundarklassen</small>
subtitle: "Zukunftsforum Bildungsforschung"
format: phheidelberg-revealjs
date: 21 11 2024
toc-depth: 1
author:
      - id: cori
        name: Richard Conrardy
        orcid: 0009-0004-2063-6898
        url: https://www.ph-heidelberg.de/mathematik/personen/promovierende/richard-conrardy/
        affiliation:
          - ref: phheidelberg
          - ref: phbern
      - id: cori
        name: Christian Spannagel
        orcid: 0
        url: https://www.ph-heidelberg.de/mathematik/personen/
        affiliation:
          - ref: phheidelberg
title-slide-attributes: 
      data-background-image: "https://www.ph-heidelberg.de/typo3conf/ext/sitepackage/Resources/Public/Images/Paedagogische_Hochschule_Heidelberg_logo.svg"
      data-background-size: 16%
      data-background-position: 98% 5%
---

# Ausgangslage

## Bildungskontext

**Schweiz**, geprägt vom *Kantönligeist* und von ...

Phasenmodellen: PADUA, AVIVA, KAKFA und SAMBA, ...

:::quelle
PADUA: [@aebli_zwolf_1983], AVIVA: [@stadeli_aviva_2020], KAFKA und SAMBA: [Reusser 1999  nach  @leuchter_rolle_2009]
:::

**Berner** (Mathematik-) Didaktik, geprägt von Heuristiken:

- SOL - Selbstorganisiertes Lernen 
- Reichhaltige Aufgaben 
- ...

:::quelle
SOL: [@hilbe_selbst_2016; @ammann-tinguely_selbst_2020]; reichhaltige Aufgaben: [@eckhart_reichhaltige_2020; @nydegger_reichhaltige_2019]
:::

# 4F-Modell und Theorie

## Empirische Forschung

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

### Studie

* Meta-Metastudie 
* *flipped classrooms*

$$n=173$$

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

### 4F, ein Phasenmodell

1.  [**Fail**]{}
2.  [**Flip**]{}
3.  [**Fix**]{}
4.  [**Feed**]{}

:::
::::

(>1500 berücksichtigt, 173 eingeschlossen)

::: quelle
[@kapur_fail_2022, S.14]
:::

## Productive **Fail**ure (PF)

> Productive Failure is an instructional approach in which students engage in **problemsolving** attempts **prior to instruction**. 

:::quelle
[@hartmann_productive_2022, S.1219]
:::

#### Design in 2 Phasen:

-   Phase 1: *generation and exploration phase*
-   Phase 2: *consolidation phase*

::: quelle
[@kapur_designing_2012, S.49]
:::

## Four core interdependant mechanisms

a) activation and differentiation of prior knowledge in relation to the targeted concepts, 
b) attention to critical conceptual features of the targeted concepts, 
c) explanation and elaboration of these features, and 
d) organization and assembly of the critical conceptual features into the targeted concepts

:::quelle
[@kapur_designing_2012]
:::

## Metastudie PF 

:::{ style="font-size: 0.5em;" }

| Moderating variables  | n   | g    | SE   |
|-----------------------|-----|------|------|
| **Fixed-effects**      | 23  | 0.28 | 0.06 |
| **Random-effects**     | 23  | 0.43 | 0.12 |
| **Education**          |     |      |      |
| Junior high            | 11  | 0.65 | 0.16 |
| High school            | 9   | 0.38 | 0.17 |
| College                | 3   | 0.06 | 0.40 |
| **Domain**             |     |      |      |
| Science/math           | 20  | 0.50 | 0.13 |
| Other                  | 3   | 0.06 | 0.40 |
| **Reported means**     |     |      |      |
| Pre/post test          | 12  | 0.19 | 0.15 |
| Adjusted post test     | 11  | 0.72 | 0.12 |
| **Duration**           |     |      |      |
| ≤ 1 week               | 16  | 0.43 | 0.16 |
| > 1 week               | 7   | 0.45 | 0.13 |
| **Researcher**         |     |      |      |
| Kapur                  | 13  | 0.85 | 0.11 |
| Others                 | 10  | 0.02 | 0.11 |

:::

:::quelle
[@darabi_learning_2018]
:::


## Flipped Classroom Method

> set of pedagogical approaches that: [...] (2) use **class time** for learning activities that are **active and social**  [...]
>
> ::: quelle
> [@abeysekera_motivation_2015, S.3]
> :::

#### Design in 2 Phasen:
* Phase 1: *learn basic content online and prior to class*
* Phase 2: *in-class time to clarify students’ understandings [...] enable students to **engage** deeply [...]*

## Engagement

### Productive Failure

"Designing the participation structures: enhancing engagement."

:::quelle
[@kapur_designing_2012, p.53]
:::

### FCM



## Mental Load & Engagement {.smaller}

|                          | Max | Productive Failure (M) | Productive Failure (SD) | Direct Instruction (M) | Direct Instruction (SD) |
|--------------------------|-----|------------------------|-------------------------|------------------------|-------------------------|
| **Mental effort (PS)**    | 9   | 7.65                   | 0.86                    | 6.18                   | 0.87                    |
| **Mental effort (I)**     | 9   | 7.38                   | 0.76                    | 6.00                   | 0.90                    |
| **Engagement (PS)**       | 5   | 4.46                   | 0.41                    | 4.39                   | 0.48                    |
| **Engagement (I)**        | 5   | 4.48                   | 0.44                    | 4.48                   | 0.49                    |

*I = instruction phase; PS = problem-solving phase.*

:::quelle
[@kapur_productive_2014, p.1013]
:::

### PF

"Productive failure students reported significantly greater mental effort than DI students during the problem-solving phase (F[1, 71] = 53.98, p < .001, d = 1.70) as well as the instruction phase (F[1, 71] = 52.09, p < .001, d = 1.66)."

:::quelle
[@kapur_productive_2014, p.1013]
:::

### FCM

“the flipped classroom approach [...] may allow better management of cognitive load”

:::quelle
[@abeysekera_motivation_2015, S.9]
:::

# Forschungsfrage & -design

## Forschungsfrage

(1) Engagement is highest during in-class activities, irrespective of variant. 
(2) Engagement predicts performance.
(3) The 4F model does not outperform the ALT model. 
(4) Mental load does not predict performance.


## Untersuchungsdesign

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
### 4F-Modell

1.  [**Fail**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
2.  [**Flip**]{style="text-decoration: underline;color: #9478b6"}
3.  [**Fix**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
4.  [**Feed**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
### Alternativmodell

1.  [**Fail (Flipped)**]{style="text-decoration: underline;color: #9478b6"}
2.  [**Instruct**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
3.  [**Fix**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
4.  [**Feed**]{style="color: #1c87ce"}
:::
::::



[synchron]{style="color: #1c87ce"}; [asynchron]{style="text-decoration: underline;color: #9478b6"}

## Forschungsfragen

1. Students in the 4F-model score higher in **engagement** than the alternative model.
2. Students in the 4F-model score higher on (a) **procedural fluency** and (b) **concept knowledge** than the alternative model.



## Untersuchungsdesign

### Pretest

Eine Woche vor dem ersten Tag der Untersuchung

| Min. | Gruppe 1      | Gruppe 2      |
|------|---------------|---------------|
| 20   | Pretest       | Pretest       |

Es findet eine zufällige Zuteilung der Klasse in zwei Gruppen statt.

<br>

### Erster Tag der Untersuchung

Klasse wird in 2 Gruppen räumlich getrennt.

| Min. | Gruppe 1      | Gruppe 2      |
|------|---------------|---------------|
| 35   | Fail koll.    | Fail ind.     |
| 5    | Mental effort | Mental effort |
| 20   | Flip          | Instruct      |
| 5    | mental effort | mental effort |
| 15   | Posttest      | Posttest      |

<br>

### Zweiter Tag der Untersuchung

Klasse wird zusammen unterrichtet.

| Min. | Gruppe 1      | Gruppe 2      |
|------|---------------|---------------|
| 35   | Fix           | Fix           |
| 5    | mental effort | mental effort |
| 20   | Feed          | Feed          |
| 5    | mental effort | mental effort |
| 15   | Posttest      | Posttest      |

# Resultate

## Bibliographie {.unlisted .scrollable}
